title: Condition number / spectral-equivalence bound for PCG convergence

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap G4 (Lemma L4, conditioning).** Claiming "PCG converges in few iterations" is not free: it requires a spectral-equivalence bound $c_1\mathcal P\preceq\mathcal A\preceq c_2\mathcal P$ giving an explicit $\kappa(\mathcal P^{-1}\mathcal A)$.

Writing $\mathcal A=\sum_{\ell=1}^{q}(\tilde Z_\ell\tilde Z_\ell^{T})\otimes(\tilde K_\ell\tilde K_\ell^{T})+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$, the observed design $\sum_\ell \tilde Z_\ell\tilde Z_\ell^T$ may be ill-conditioned or rank-deficient under arbitrary missingness $\Omega$, so spectral equivalence is **not automatic**.

**Required:** derive an honest bound on $\kappa(\mathcal P^{-1}\mathcal A)$ (e.g. in terms of $\lambda$, $\max_\ell\|K[a_\ell,:]\|^2$, and properties/leverage of the observed rows), and feed it into the CG iteration count $O(\sqrt\kappa\log(1/\varepsilon))$. If no data-independent bound exists, state the assumption under which fast convergence holds.
